description_long: This object is a classic lever-action shotgun with a long, blued steel barrel and a polished wooden stock. The stock features a rich brown finish with visible wood grain and subtle engraved detailing. The receiver is metallic, likely brass or steel, and connects the barrel to the stock, with a prominent lever loop beneath for cycling the action. The barrel is round and slender, with a front sight bead and a tubular magazine running parallel beneath it. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses a single trigger, with the lever forming part of the guard's lower boundary. The overall design is reminiscent of late 19th-century firearms, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A lever-action shotgun with a long blued barrel, polished wooden stock, and metallic receiver, featuring a rounded trigger guard and engraved woodwork.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the wooden stock, engraved details, metallic receiver, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Front-on view displaying the circular muzzle of the barrel and the narrow profile of the shotgun.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the lever, trigger guard, and the other side of the engraved wooden stock.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and receiver.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, round, with a tubular magazine beneath and a front sight bead; no ribbing or venting visible
attribute_trigger_guard_design: Rounded, medium-sized trigger guard integrated with the lever, smooth with no finger grooves or reinforcement
